what_it_does: Install and operational reference for the per-ear HTTP speech-to-text services under voice/ears, documenting ports, venv setup, model sources, environment variables, shared HTTP contract, measured VRAM constraints, and boot default.
observations: Three GPU STT ears (parakeet, canary, granite) each run as a separate HTTP service in its own Python venv on its own port to avoid dependency conflicts.
All ears share the same HTTP contract: POST /inference with multipart file upload returns {"text":"..."}; GET / and /health return liveness JSON.
A measured VRAM ceiling of only 654 MiB free on a 16 GB card is treated as an environment blocker, not a code gap, and is marked needs_tim.
The HF model repos are already present in the local cache.
Lane 5 adds ONNX-runtime ears (moonshine, parakeet-onnx) that default to CPU and require no torch/NeMo.
The boot default remains whispercpp; switching to another ear is a config/registry change only after verification.
Adding or flipping an ear is intended to require only a STT_PROVIDERS row and config change, never a code edit.
standouts: The 654 MiB free VRAM measurement is the gating fact that prevents any GPU ear from loading.
Each GPU ear is deliberately isolated in its own venv and port due to NeMo/transformers/torch conflicts.
Lane 5 ONNX ears are presented as a lean, CPU-first realtime alternative.
whispercpp remains the default until another ear is verified up and VRAM is re-measured.
conventions: Living reference markdown with YAML frontmatter and module tags.
Per-service install snippets include exact venv path, port, and pip commands.
Environment variables follow the COMPANY_<EAR>_<OPTION> naming pattern.
Fail-loud 500 responses with error type and message.
Registry-driven selection through voice/stt.py rather than direct code edits.
concerns: Install snippets hardcode the absolute path /home/tim/company/... which is user- and host-specific.
The VRAM measurement is dated and environment-specific; it may stale quickly without a re-run process.
The CUDA-13 GPU hazard is noted but left to manual verification rather than an automated check.
Canary's heavy memory footprint warning lacks concrete co-residency scheduling guidance.
notes: This file is the operator runbook for standing up and switching between isolated STT ears, with a prominent warning that current GPU memory is too low to load any GPU ear.
questions: What exactly do _stt_service.py and voice/stt.py implement for the registry and transport?
Who or what re-runs the VRAM measurement and clears the needs_tim blocker?
What does 'the bridge / loop' refer to in this context?
What is the meaning of the acronym STA in Lane 5?
How are model license and commercial-OK claims verified and tracked?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose is a per-ear install reference, and the content matches that while also covering runtime contract, registry integration, and the current environment blocker.
intention_signals: It tries to make STT ears pluggable infrastructure: each ear is a standalone HTTP service with an identical contract, selected through a registry, while honestly surfacing that the current host GPU is too full to run them.
novelty: It explicitly treats a VRAM shortage as an environment blocker (needs_tim) rather than a code gap, and documents a separate ONNX 'Lane 5' as a lean realtime tier alongside the GPU ears.
contribution: A single-file operator reference that records how to install, start, and switch each isolated STT ear, the shared HTTP contract they must implement, the environment variables that control them, and the current GPU-memory blocker that prevents their use.
summary_for_embedding: Install and operations reference for isolated HTTP speech-to-text ear services (parakeet, canary, granite, moonshine-onnx, parakeet-onnx) under company/voice/ears, including shared API contract, environment variables, VRAM constraints, and boot-default guidance.
intention_for_embedding: To give operators a living runbook for adding, starting, and selecting STT ears via configuration rather than code changes, while flagging the current environment as blocked for GPU models.
